query: can exosomes be analyzed with FACS

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Types of Flow Cytometers
retrieved_docs.content: Cell sorters: This type of flow cytometer can segregate a specific cell population based on their specific immunoexpression. This cell population is collected in separate tubes or slides. Most cell sorters use electrostatic methods, also known as jet-in-air, whereby the stream exiting the flow cell is vibrated to generate droplets. The objective is to isolate single cells inside individual droplets. When a target cell is identified, a positive or negative electrostatic charge is applied to the droplet. The target droplet is deflected by charged plates and deposited in a collection tube. Uncharged droplets are directed to a waste receptacle. [22] Other cell sorters use different cell isolation methods, such as air diversion for large particles and microfluidic actuators to divert cells of interest inside a contained apparatus. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Extracellular Vesicles -- Introduction -- Characterization
retrieved_docs.content: After their isolation, it is necessary to confirm that the required population of EVs has been obtained. Scientists utilize several techniques to do this confirmation. First is the electron microscope. The regular scanning electron microscope shows EVs as irregular shaped membrane-enclosed vesicles. Alternatively, cryo-electron microscopy can be utilized. Despite being time-consuming and expensive, it gives more accurate information about the shape and size of the isolated vesicles. The second method of characterization is nano-track analysis, which provides a general idea about the concentration and size distribution of the isolated vesicles. The third method is a western blot, which is used to recognize protein markers associated with the membrane of the EVs. Despite some distinction of specific proteins on the surfaces of different EVs, there is still no consensus on which proteins can be used reliably as markers for the different types. [4] Recently, flow cytometry procedures have been developed to characterize different types of EVs based on their surface markers. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Biomarker Assays for Elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen Risk Analysis -- Testing Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: Exosome precipitation occurs as a result of further ultracentrifugation of the supernatant. The ExoDx Intelliscore is far more patient-friendly compared to other biomarkers, as it does not require a digital rectal exam or prostatic massage. Unlike other urine-based tests for prostate cancer, it is available as a kit for home usage that patients can mail directly back to the diagnostic laboratory. [73]

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer -- Evaluation -- Post-Prostate-Specific Antigen Pre-Biopsy Prostate Cancer Bioassay Risk Stratification Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Predictive bioassay testing that includes clinical variables, such as SelectMDx and 4K, has sometimes been considered more reliable than tests that do not, such as PHI, ExoDx Prostate Intelliscore  exosome, and PCA3. [140] However, these tests that require significant clinical patient information to achieve their competitive NPV scores are, by definition, less reliable when used alone and are not truly independent as they rely on clinical data and computer algorithms for statistical validation. As they depend on the clinical information to provide a substantial part of their final conclusion, the test is incomplete and invalid by itself without the addition of the patient data and the computer algorithms. When the clinician uses their clinical judgment based on the same clinical patient information used in formulating the bioassay test result, factors such as age, PSA level, PSA density, and prior biopsies are counted twice, giving them disproportionate weight and bias.

retrieved_docs.title: Biomarker Assays for Elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen Risk Analysis -- Testing Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: Most urinary biomarker assays are performed after prostatic manipulation (massage), except the ExoDx EPI score, which is not required. Studies have shown that a DRE can enrich urinary biomarkers and enhance yield, the first void after a DRE is obtained and analyzed. Following a prostate massage, the first 10 to 50 mL of urine that is voided are centrifuged at 1000 to 2000 rotations per minute to produce precipitates of urine that may contain prostate cancer cells, cell fragments, and various biomolecules such as DNA, RNA, and proteins from these cells. Free proteins, DNA, RNA, other tiny molecules, and exosomes are all detected in the supernatant.

retrieved_docs.content: Flow cytometric analysis of the body fluids with atypical cells, especially those of hematolymphoid origin, can be rewarding. [12] The approach may remain the same as in peripheral blood samples and bone marrow.

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Types of Flow Cytometers
retrieved_docs.content: Mass cytometers: The cells are labeled with heavy metal ion-tagged antibodies, and the cell population is analyzed using time-of-flight spectrometry. Fluorescence-based antibodies are not used. [26] The strength of this technology is its ability to use a broad array of stable isotopes, thereby eliminating the limitations caused by spectral overlap typically encountered with fluorochromes. [21]

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Types of Flow Cytometers
retrieved_docs.content: Acoustic focusing cytometers: These cytometers use ultrasonic waves to enhance cell focusing for laser interrogation, significantly improving the efficiency and accuracy of flow cytometry. Acoustic focusing technology accommodates a wider variety of cell sizes, from small platelets to larger cells such as cardiomyocytes. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer -- Evaluation -- Risk Stratification Bioassay Tests
retrieved_docs.content: ExoDx Prostate Intelliscore or Exosome test: This test  uses PCA3 and urinary TMPRSS2:ERG to detect clinically significant prostate cancer. The test analyzes exosomal RNA for 3 biomarkers known to be expressed in the urine of men with high-grade prostate cancer. A proprietary algorithm is then used to assign a risk score that predicts the presence of high-grade (Gleason Score=7 or higher, or any Gleason Grade Pattern 4 or 5) prostate cancer. Unlike other urine-based tests for prostate cancer, no DRE or prostatic massage is required, and a kit for home use kit is available, making testing much more convenient for patients. The NPV is 91.3%, with a sensitivity rating of 91.9%. [141] The ExoDx exosome test performs particularly well for Gleason 4+3=7 patients, with an NPV of 97% in this group. [142]

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Types of Flow Cytometers
retrieved_docs.content: Flow cytometers can be classified into several types based on their functionality and complexity.

retrieved_docs.title: Biomarker Assays for Elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen Risk Analysis -- Diagnostic Tests -- Urine-Based Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: ExoDx p rostate intelliscore: Exosomes are tiny extracellular vesicles (40-150 nm) released by various cell types, including cancerous cells. They contain lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids. Prostate-derived exosomes serve as diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers as they are a source of proteins and microRNA. [94] [95] Urinary exosome analysis offers benefits, including stable exosomes owing to their exosomal lipid bilayer, which protects the genetic content from enzymatic destruction. No unpleasant prostatic massage is needed before collecting the sample, and the test can be performed at home with the specimen mailed directly to the laboratory by the patient, saving staff time and paperwork. Exosomal RNA and protein content that are tested may be superior to less stable, chemical, nongenetic-based biomarkers.

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Quality Management and Controls in Flow Cytometry
retrieved_docs.content: A written QC policy is essential, outlining clear objectives, detailed procedures, documentation guidelines, and a process for regular review and updates. Implementing a comprehensive QC system in flow cytometry ensures reliable and accurate assay results, enhancing diagnostic capabilities and improving patient care. [38]

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Preparation -- Specimen Requirements and Procedure
retrieved_docs.content: Once the experiment is set up in the flow cytometer, all pre-analytical checks are conducted, and the sample is loaded into the flow cytometer. For optimal analysis, acquiring a minimum of 10 5 to 10 7 cells at a rate of 30,000 events/second is recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Equipment -- Instrument and Principle
retrieved_docs.content: Flow cytometers generate data in the FCS (flow cytometry standard) file format. FCS files contain many keywords that can be used during analysis. Some of these are required by the file standard and autopopulated by the manufacturer's software. Other keywords require input from the user. [31]

retrieved_docs.title: Flow Cytometry Blood Cell Identification -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Immunophenotyping is the most common application of flow cytometry. Using multiple immunomarkers, flow cytometry can be used for multiple purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 977
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn Screening Terminology is a categorical framework that incorporates several obstetric and perinatal concepts. Within its subset, it includes the Arabic terms مقدمات تسمم الحمل (preliminary signs of preeclampsia), تسمم الحمل (preeclampsia), and قَيْء الحمل خفيف الوطأة (mild hyperemesis gravidarum), reflecting maternal conditions that can affect newborn health and screening outcomes. Additionally, the subset of Newborn Screening Terminology encompasses the concept الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين (delivery by fetal suction or vacuum‑assisted extraction), highlighting a specific mode of birth that may be relevant to newborn assessment and follow‑up. Together, these concepts illustrate how Newborn Screening Terminology integrates maternal disease states and delivery techniques to inform comprehensive newborn evaluation and care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 2 -- Community 570
retrieved_docs.content: preterm birth, chorioamnionitis), maternal factors (cervical pathology, ectopy), delivery modes (cesarean, forceps, vacuum‑assisted, extraction), fetal findings (missed abortion, low birth weight), and pregnancy classifications (selective, optional termination), providing a comprehensive framework for perinatal screening, clinical decision‑making, and management of maternal‑fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 2 -- Community 1231
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn Screening Terminology is organized to include a specific subset called plod, and within this plod subset the concepts الولادة بالملقط (forceps delivery) and الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين (vacuum‑extraction delivery) are incorporated, indicating that the terminology for newborn screening explicitly encompasses these particular delivery methods as relevant elements of its classification system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2069
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction is a broad medical concept that refers to the removal of a tissue, organ, or fetus, and the specific obstetric technique of الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين (vacuum‑assisted delivery) represents a narrower, specialized form of this procedure. In this context, الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين is understood as a particular type of extraction employed to assist fetal delivery by applying suction, illustrating how a general extraction principle can be applied in a specific clinical scenario.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 1021
retrieved_docs.content: organizes general obstetric procedure categories (إجراء توليدي, Antepartum Procedures, Obstetrics incision) and specific clinical actions—ranging from diagnostic sampling and membrane management to fetal stimulation, reduction of multiple gestations, and surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancies—providing a comprehensive framework for coordinating obstetric care across the ante‑partum, intrapartum, and postpartum periods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2167
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn Screening Terminology is organized to include a specific subset called plod, and this plod subset encompasses the concept described by the Arabic term “الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين,” which refers to delivery by fetal suction. This structure illustrates how the terminology for newborn screening categorizes related concepts within defined subsets for clearer classification and reference.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2420
retrieved_docs.content: Amnioinfusion and خزعة الزغابات المشيمية (chorionic villus sampling) are both categorized as Antepartum application, insertion or removal procedures. These procedures are performed before delivery and involve the insertion of instruments or substances into the maternal‑fetal environment for therapeutic or diagnostic purposes. Amnioinfusion specifically refers to the infusion of fluid into the amniotic cavity to alleviate conditions such as oligohydramnios or improve fetal monitoring, while خزعة الزغابات المشيمية involves the sampling of placental tissue to obtain fetal genetic material for early prenatal diagnosis. Both interventions are considered child procedures within the broader class of antepartum application, insertion or removal procedures, highlighting their role in prenatal care and fetal assessment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2317
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة, and الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة is clinically associated with بضع الرحم. This indicates that an unidentified type of caesarean section scar may increase the risk of early placental separation, which in turn is linked to uterine atony, underscoring the need for vigilant obstetric assessment in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 205
retrieved_docs.content: concepts such as forceps and vacuum‑extraction deliveries, and links maternal abnormal cervical smear (لطاخة عنق الرحم غير طبيعية) with placenta previa, high‑risk pregnancy, and low birth‑weight outcomes. Together, these entities and their interrelationships illustrate a comprehensive framework for obstetric classification, risk stratification, procedural variation, and integrated maternal‑fetal care across clinical databases, the SMQ, and newborn screening systems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: Amnioinfusion, بزل السائل الأمنيوسي, and خزعة الزغابات المشيمية are all classified under the category of antepartum application, insertion or removal procedures. These interventions are performed before delivery to modify, sample, or assess the amniotic environment. Amnioinfusion involves the introduction of fluid into the amniotic cavity, بزل السائل الأمنيوسي (amniocentesis) entails aspiration of amniotic fluid for diagnostic testing, and خزعة الزغابات المشيمية (chorionic villus sampling) involves obtaining placental tissue for genetic analysis. Recognizing that these procedures share a common therapeutic and diagnostic purpose helps clinicians select appropriate antepartum interventions and coordinate patient care.

Operations to facilitate delivery are defined as types of cesarean delivery, reinforcing the central role of surgical birth techniques. Together, these interconnected entities and relationships provide a comprehensive framework for understanding the clinical, procedural, and data‑driven aspects of labor, delivery, and related obstetric conditions, supporting informed decision‑making and optimized patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2062
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with both الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة (early placental separation) and مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor). In addition, REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR is clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان, indicating that an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and a poor reproductive history are both risk factors for preterm labor, while the unknown‑type scar also predisposes to early placental separation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2820
retrieved_docs.content: INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS are classified as subprocedures within the broader categories of فصادة الكريات البيض (white‑blood‑cell apheresis) and نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion). Both parent procedures involve the manipulation, collection, or reinfusion of blood components, and the inclusion of INJECTIONS AND IMPLANTATIONS under these headings underscores their role in delivering therapeutic agents or devices directly into the patient’s circulatory system or tissues as part of blood‑related therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 4 -- Community 2857
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction is classified as a form of surgical manipulation and serves as a broader category that encompasses the specific procedure الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين. The relationship between Extraction and الولادة عن طريق شفط الجنين is narrower, indicating that the latter represents a particular type of extraction technique. Additionally, Extraction is employed as a method in vaginal cesarean section, linking this surgical approach to both the general concept of surgical manipulation and the specific fetal‑suction delivery technique.
